## Source Record

- Source URL: `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot`
- Fetch method: `webfetch` in markdown mode
- Capture date: `2026-04-14`

## Why It Matters

This page shows GitHub Copilot as a broad platform, not just IDE autocomplete. The index spans chat, cloud agents, CLI agents, SDK usage, MCP, skills, memory, code review, model choice, billing, and organization or enterprise administration.

## Key Excerpts

> "You can use GitHub Copilot to enhance your productivity and assist as you work on code."

> "About GitHub Copilot cloud agent" describes an agent that can research a repository, create an implementation plan, make code changes on a branch, and open a pull request.

> The index exposes dedicated docs for `Copilot CLI`, `Copilot SDK`, `Model Context Protocol (MCP)`, `custom agents`, `agent skills`, and `agentic memory`.

> The same index also links policy and administration surfaces for model access, premium request management, feature governance, and enterprise controls.

## Capture Notes

- The fetched page is mostly a large navigation index rather than a single narrative document.
- The main signal is scope: GitHub positions Copilot as a multi-surface agent platform with both end-user and admin workflows.
- The raw fetch was truncated, but the top-level structure and linked capabilities were still clear.
